concepts.cultural_hint: A traditional conical Assamese jaapi woven from bamboo and tokou palm leaves, displayed without any writing.
A white Assamese handwoven gamosa with a red woven border, folded over a simple bamboo chair.
A traditional Assamese bell-metal xorai offering tray with its domed lid, placed on a woven mat.
A conical handwoven bamboo fish trap beside a village pond, shown as the single dominant object.
Fresh areca nuts gathered beneath a supari palm in an Assamese homestead garden, with no packaging or text.
A small unglazed clay cup of hot tea on a weathered wooden bench at a local roadside tea stall.
A traditional Assamese dhol used during Bihu, resting against a bamboo wall with its straps visible.
A simple Assamese rice meal served neatly on a fresh banana leaf in a domestic setting.
